let noStoreFetchIdx = 0

async function cacheNewResult<T>(
  result: T,
  incrementalCache: IncrementalCache,
  cacheKey: string,
  tags: string[],
  revalidate: number | false | undefined,
  fetchIdx: number,
  fetchUrl: string
): Promise<unknown> {
  await incrementalCache.set(
    cacheKey,
    {
      kind: CachedRouteKind.FETCH,
      data: {
        headers: {},
        // TODO: handle non-JSON values?
        body: JSON.stringify(result),
        status: 200,
        url: '',
      } satisfies CachedFetchData,
      revalidate:
        typeof revalidate !== 'number' ? CACHE_ONE_YEAR_SECONDS : revalidate,
    },
    { fetchCache: true, tags, fetchIdx, fetchUrl }
  )
  return
}

/home/user/workspace/node_modules/next/dist/esm/server/after/after.js.map:1:{"version":3,"sources":["../../../../src/server/after/after.ts"],"sourcesContent":["import { workAsyncStorage } from '../app-render/work-async-storage.external'

export type AfterTask<T = unknown> = Promise<T> | AfterCallback<T>
export type AfterCallback<T = unknown> = () => T | Promise<T>

/**
 * This function allows you to schedule callbacks to be executed after the current request finishes.
 */
export function after<T>(task: AfterTask<T>): void {
  const workStore = workAsyncStorage.getStore()

  if (!workStore) {
    // TODO(after): the linked docs page talks about *dynamic* APIs, which after soon won't be anymore
    throw new Error(
      '`after` was called outside a request scope. Read more: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/next-dynamic-api-wrong-context'
    )
  }

  const { afterContext } = workStore
  return afterContext.after(task)
}

1 import { revalidateTag } from 'next/cache'; 2 import { after } from 'next/server'; 3 import { getCachedProducts } from '@/lib/products'; 4 import { performCatalogSync } from '@/lib/sync'; 5 import { CatalogTable } from '@/components/admin/catalog-table'; 6 import { SyncForm } from '@/components/admin/sync-form'; 7 import type { SyncState } from '@/components/admin/sync-form'; 8 9 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 10 /* Metadata */ 11 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 12 13 export const metadata = { 14 title: 'Catalog — Admin', 15 }; 16 17 /** 18 * Render on every request so cache-tag revalidation takes effect. 19 * The data layer (`unstable_cache` / `"products"` tag) prevents 20 * redundant DB queries between syncs. 21 */ 22 export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'; 23 24 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 25 /* Inline Server Action — sync trigger */ 26 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 27 28 /** 29 * Triggered by the "Sync Latest Catalog" form submission. 30 * 31 * Design decisions 32 * ---------------- 33 * 1. **Fire-and-forget via `after()`** — the action returns immediately 34 * so the admin can keep browsing while the sync runs in the background. 35 * Product data is briefly stale, which is acceptable for the admin use 36 * case (operators prefer a responsive UI over blocking on fresh data). 37 * 38 * 2. **Tag-based cache invalidation** — once the sync completes, the 39 * `"products"` cache tag is fully expired. Any subsequent page render 40 * that reads through `getCachedProducts` (wrapped with `unstable_cache` 41 * and tagged `"products"`) will fetch fresh data from the DB. 42 * 43 * 3. **Eventual consistency** — because all catalog views use the same 44 * cache tag, invalidating `"products"` guarantees consistency across 45 * the entire admin surface once the cache is repopulated. 46 */ 47 async function syncCatalog( 48 _prevState: SyncState | null, 49 _formData: FormData 50 ): Promise { 51 'use server'; 52 53 try { 54 // Schedule the sync to run after the response is sent. 55 // The admin sees the success message immediately and can continue 56 // working. The product list may be briefly stale. 57 after(async () => { 58 await performCatalogSync(); 59 // Fully expire all cache entries tagged "products" so the next 60 // page load fetches fresh data from the DB. 61 revalidateTag('products', 'max'); 62 }); 63 64 return { 65 success: true, 66 message: 67 'Catalog sync started — changes will appear across all catalog views shortly.', 68 }; 69 } catch (err) { 70 console.error('Catalog sync failed:', err); 71 return { 72 success: false, 73 message: 74 err instanceof Error 75 ? err.message 76 : 'An unexpected error occurred while syncing the catalog.', 77 }; 78 } 79 } 80 81 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 82 /* Page component */ 83 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */ 84 85 export default async function CatalogPage() { 86 const products = await getCachedProducts(); 87 88 return ( 89

1	import { unstable_cache } from 'next/cache';
2	import { getAllProducts } from '@/lib/db';
3	import type { Product } from '@/types/product';
4	
5	/**
6	 * Returns the full product catalog, cached under the "products" tag.
7	 *
8	 * The cache is NOT time-based — it only expires when the "products" tag
9	 * is revalidated via `revalidateTag('products', 'max')` (triggered by
10	 * the admin sync action). This keeps the read-heavy catalog fast while
11	 * still allowing explicit cache busting after an upstream sync.
12	 */
13	export const getCachedProducts = unstable_cache(
14	  async (): Promise<Product[]> => {
15	    const raw = await getAllProducts();
16	    // The db mock already returns well-shaped data, but in a real app
17	    // this is where you'd normalise / transform before caching.
18	    return raw as Product[];
19	  },
20	  ['catalog-all-products'],
21	  {
22	    tags: ['products'],
23	    /**
24	     * `revalidate: false` means the cache entry lives until explicitly
25	     * invalidated via `revalidateTag('products', …)`.  No stale-while-
26	     * revalidate window.
27	     */
28	    revalidate: false,
29	  }
30	);
